---
title: "Project Overview"
format: html
---

## Understanding FHIR (Fast Healthcare Interoperability Resources)

FHIR (Fast Healthcare Interoperability Resources) is a standard framework created by HL7 designed to facilitate the exchange of electronic health records. Unlike older standards, FHIR is built on modern web technologies—specifically a RESTful API—making it easier for different healthcare systems to share data seamlessly. It breaks down clinical data into modular "Resources," such as Patients, Encounters, and Observations. By using a standardized format, healthcare providers can ensure that data captured in one system is accurately interpreted by another, which is critical for patient safety and data-driven clinical decision-making.

---

## Initial Population Analysis

The first step in our analysis is determining the "density" of these FHIR resources. This visualization shows what percentage of our total patient population has at least one record of a specific resource type.

![FHIR Resource Density by Type](Resource_Density.png)

**Key Insight:** While basic administrative and primary care data (Encounters and Immunizations) are present for nearly 100% of the population, specialized diagnostic resources like Imaging Studies are significantly less common, appearing in only about 21% of patient files.
